capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The travel desk is reconciling two itineraries before the approval queue closes. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable trip registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the trip named “Seoul Trade Delegation” at “Seoul” and the trip named “Taipei Standards Forum” at “Taipei”. Every reported value must trace through the registry: search by the exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first trip-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Write the complete reply to audit_reply.txt as newline-terminated plain text. Keep it under 75 words, include both stable IDs, compare the two retrieved status values, disclose no stale or unrelated match, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.” After writing the file, return exactly the same text as the final response.
